name: Miva customers to NetSuite customers
description:
This flow exports customer data from Miva's ecommerce platform and imports it into NetSuite. It can create new customer records or update existing ones in NetSuite based on the email field. 

Summary:
Overall, this flow exports customers from Miva, filters the exported data based on the last run timestamp, and imports the customers into NetSuite, either creating new records or updating existing ones based on the email field.

Technical Details:
The flow has two parts: "pageGenerators" and "pageProcessors". In the "pageGenerators" section, the Miva assistant is used to export modified customer data from Miva. The response is transformed and filtered to exclude previously exported records. In the "pageProcessors" section, the exported data is imported into NetSuite using the NetSuiteDistributedImport adaptor. Field mapping and data modification are defined in the configuration. Overall, this flow synchronizes customer data between Miva and NetSuite.

This flow is called "Miva customers to NetSuite customers" and it exports customers from Miva's ecommerce platform and posts them to NetSuite. The flow can either create new customer records in NetSuite or update existing ones. The matching of existing customers is done based on the email field.

The flow consists of two parts: "pageGenerators" and "pageProcessors". 

In the "pageGenerators" section, there is a single source called "Get Miva customers". This source is configured to export customers from Miva that have been modified since the last run of the flow. The export is done using the Miva assistant and the "customerlist_load_query" operation. The export is performed using either the HTTP or REST method, depending on the configuration. The response from Miva is transformed using an expression. Additionally, there is a filter expression that is used to exclude records that were exported in previous runs of the flow.

In the "pageProcessors" section, there is a single destination called "Import NetSuite customers". This destination is configured to import the exported Miva customers into NetSuite. The import operation is set to "add", which means that new customer records will be created in NetSuite. The import is performed using the NetSuiteDistributedImport adaptor. The mapping of fields from the Miva customers to the NetSuite customers is defined in the "mapping" field. There are also two addressbook lists defined in the mapping for billing and shipping addresses. Additionally, there is a preMap hook function defined that modifies the data before it is mapped.
